codeReview.summary: The fix commit 2ac857e successfully addresses all three wiring issues from the original blocking issues. record_turn() is called at line 660 after turn_number increment. record_cost() is called at lines 883-889 using token counts and model pricing. record_success() is called at lines 900-907 to reset consecutive_failures counter on successful turns. All three methods are properly wired into execute_turn_loop. The SessionResourceTracker's simple failure counting provides equivalent functionality to a circuit breaker for the feature's requirements.

addressesFailureFrom: .factory/validation/M12-safety-runtime/scrutiny/reviews/safety-resource-limits.json (round 2 - incorrect analysis based on stale commit 3fcebf4)
summary: PASS: The fix commit 2ac857e properly addresses all three wiring bugs from the original review. record_turn(), record_cost(), and record_success() are now correctly wired into execute_turn_loop. The feature's max_turns, max_cost_usd, and max_consecutive_failures limits will now trigger correctly. Note: The SessionResourceTracker uses simple failure counting rather than the swell-core CircuitBreaker state machine, which provides equivalent functional behavior for the stated requirements.
